content.problemStatement:
In this task, you’ll determine how much warmer City A is compared to City B by subtracting their temperatures.

**Temperature Values**

• City A: 75 degrees

• City B: 60 degrees

If you subtract 60 from 75, the result will be:

`15
`Try completing the code to get the temperature difference, then run it to confirm your answer!
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 1000), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers A and B (−1000 ≤ A, B ≤ 1000), the temperatures of City A and City B respectively.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the integer value A − B.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is one test case. The difference 75 − 60 equals 15, which is printed.

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases T. For each test case read the two integers A and B, compute their difference (A - B) using ordinary integer arithmetic, and output the result. No preprocessing or complex data structures are required.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in constant time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are stored regardless of T.
